samples.name: improving_week_growth_tier
samples.description: Week-over-week ROAS up 18%, CTR up 12% — narrative should call out the trend and recommend modest scale.
samples.stubbed_output: {"headline":"ROAS up 18%, conversions up 26% — recommend small scale","summary":"Three of four metrics improved over last week. ROAS climbed to 3.2 from 2.7 on flat spend, and conversion count grew faster than spend (26% vs 3%), indicating real audience receptivity rather than budget effect.","recommendation":"Increase daily budget by 15% on the top-performing campaign. Re-check in 7 days; if frequency stays under 2.0 the room to scale is there.","confidence":"high"}

samples.name: declining_week_recommend_pause
samples.description: ROAS down 35%, frequency creeping over 2.5 — narrative should flag creative fatigue + recommend refresh.
samples.stubbed_output: {"headline":"ROAS down 35%, creative fatigue showing","summary":"CTR dropped from 1.0% to 0.6% over the same audience as frequency climbed from 2.1 to 2.8 — classic audience saturation pattern. Spend stayed flat but conversion count fell 43%, so the audience isn\u2019t responding to the current creative.","recommendation":"Refresh the top two creatives this week. Don\u2019t scale until CTR recovers above 1.0% and frequency drops below 2.0.","confidence":"high"}
